data.narrative:
Kela is a Series A–stage Defense & Security company founded by Hamutal Meridor, Alon Dror, Omer Bar-Ilan, and Jason Manne, having raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression anchors to the $100M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $200M. Peer-median analysis examines 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). The median valuation across this set is $300M, with a range of $150M–$500M.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $170M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $250M. The stage-progression method implies lower end bias due to reliance on historical capital raised, while peer comps skew higher, reflecting market pricing for similar-stage defense-tech assets. Key uncertainties include revenue generation timing, customer concentration in government contracting, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer set. The $250M median represents a reasonable central estimate pending final commercial and cap-table diligence.
